words.definitionEn: the state or process of being destroyed or severely damaged
words.exampleEn: A large number of churches fell into ruin after the revolution.

words.definitionEn: to damage something so badly that it loses all its value, pleasure, etc.
words.exampleEn: The bad weather ruined our trip.

words.definitionEn: a room or rooms in a building, partly or completely below the level of the ground
words.exampleEn: Kitchen goods are sold in the basement.

words.definitionEn: connected with or like the countryside
words.exampleEn: rural areas

words.definitionEn: a sudden strong movement or action
words.exampleEn: rush for something, Shoppers made a rush for the exits.

words.definitionEn: to move or to do something with great speed, often too fast
words.exampleEn: We've got plenty of time; there's no need to rush.

words.definitionEn: in the most important ways, without considering things that are less important
words.exampleEn: I think we are basically saying the same thing.

words.definitionEn: to try a small amount of a particular food to see what it is like; to experience something for a short time to see what it is like
words.exampleEn: I sampled the delights of Greek cooking for the first time.

words.definitionEn: an electronic device that is sent into space and moves around the earth or another planet. It is used for communicating by radio, television, etc. and for gathering information.
words.exampleEn: a weather/communications/spy satellite

words.definitionEn: the good feeling that you have when you have achieved something or when something that you wanted to happen does happen; something that gives you this feeling
words.exampleEn: to gain/get/derive satisfaction from something

words.definitionEn: pleased because you have achieved something or because something that you wanted to happen has happened
words.exampleEn: a satisfied customer

words.definitionEn: to make somebody pleased by doing or giving them what they want
words.exampleEn: Nothing satisfies him—he's always complaining.

words.definitionEn: a container for holding or carrying things. Baskets are made of long thin pieces of material that bends and twists easily, for example plastic, wire or wicker.
words.exampleEn: a shopping basket

words.definitionEn: an amount of something such as time or money that you do not need to use or spend
words.exampleEn: saving of something, Buy three and make a saving of 55p.

words.definitionEn: the size or extent of something, especially when compared with something else
words.exampleEn: on a … scale, They entertain on a large scale (= they hold expensive parties with a lot of guests).

words.definitionEn: behaviour or an event that people think is morally or legally wrong and causes public feelings of shock or anger
words.exampleEn: a series of sex scandals

words.definitionEn: a situation in which a lot of people are anxious or frightened about something
words.exampleEn: a health scare

words.definitionEn: to frighten somebody
words.exampleEn: scare somebody, You scared me.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to keep or check financial accounts
words.exampleEn: We talked to the company’s chief accountant.

words.definitionEn: a description of how things might happen in the future
words.exampleEn: Let me suggest a possible scenario.

words.definitionEn: to arrange for something to happen at a particular time
words.exampleEn: schedule something for something, The meeting is scheduled for Friday afternoon.

words.definitionEn: a plan or system for doing or organizing something
words.exampleEn: a training scheme

words.definitionEn: a person who knows a lot about a particular subject because they have studied it in detail
words.exampleEn: a classical scholar

words.definitionEn: an amount of money given to somebody by an organization to help pay for their education
words.exampleEn: She won a scholarship to study at Stanford.

words.definitionEn: a mark, a cut or an injury made by scratching somebody’s skin or the surface of something
words.exampleEn: Her hands were covered in scratches from the brambles.

words.definitionEn: to rub your skin with your nails, usually because it is itching
words.exampleEn: scratch something, John yawned and scratched his chin.

words.definitionEn: a loud high shout made by somebody who is hurt, frightened, excited, etc.; a loud high noise
words.exampleEn: scream of something, She let out a scream of pain.

words.definitionEn: to give a loud, high shout, because you are hurt, frightened, excited, etc.
words.exampleEn: There was a bang and I heard someone scream.

words.definitionEn: a piece of wood with a handle, made in various shapes and sizes, and used for hitting the ball in games such as baseball, cricket and table tennis
words.exampleEn: a baseball/cricket bat

words.definitionEn: to show a film, etc. in a cinema or on television
words.exampleEn: a list of films to be screened as part of the festival

words.definitionEn: the act of showing a film or television programme
words.exampleEn: This will be the movie's first screening in this country.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody a place to sit; to sit down in a place
words.exampleEn: Please wait to be seated (= in a restaurant, etc.).

words.definitionEn: a part of an area of activity, especially of a country’s economy
words.exampleEn: the service/banking/manufacturing/financial sector

words.definitionEn: likely to continue or be successful for a long time
words.exampleEn: a secure job/income

words.definitionEn: to obtain or achieve something, especially when this means using a lot of effort
words.exampleEn: secure something, to secure a contract/deal/funding

words.definitionEn: to look for something/somebody
words.exampleEn: seek something/somebody, Drivers are advised to seek alternative routes.

words.definitionEn: a person who is trying to find or get the thing mentioned
words.exampleEn: an attention/a publicity seeker

words.definitionEn: to choose somebody/something from a group of people or things, usually according to a system
words.exampleEn: select somebody/something for something, He hasn't been selected for the team.

words.definitionEn: the process of choosing somebody/something from a group of people or things, usually according to a system
words.exampleEn: She took a long time to make her selection.

words.definitionEn: the type of person you are, especially the way you normally behave, look or feel
words.exampleEn: You'll soon be feeling your old self again (= feeling well or happy again).

words.definitionEn: a class at a university or college when a small group of students and a teacher discuss or study a particular topic
words.exampleEn: Teaching is by lectures and seminars.

words.definitionEn: high in rank or status; higher in rank or status than others
words.exampleEn: a senior official/officer/manager/executive

words.definitionEn: to become aware of something even though you cannot see it, hear it, etc.
words.exampleEn: sense something, Sensing danger, they started to run.

words.definitionEn: aware of and able to understand other people and their feelings
words.exampleEn: a sensitive and caring man

words.definitionEn: to say officially in court that somebody is to receive a particular punishment
words.exampleEn: sentence somebody to something, He sentenced the defendant to life in prison.

words.definitionEn: to try very hard to achieve something difficult or to deal with something unpleasant or dangerous
words.exampleEn: Both teams battled hard.

words.definitionEn: a set of events, actions, numbers, etc. which have a particular order and which lead to a particular result
words.exampleEn: He described the sequence of events leading up to the robbery.

words.definitionEn: a period of time that is spent doing a particular activity
words.exampleEn: a training/practice session

words.definitionEn: to put an end to an argument or a disagreement
words.exampleEn: settle something, to settle a dispute/a matter/an issue

words.definitionEn: a person who goes to live in a new country or region
words.exampleEn: Most of the settlers came from England.

words.definitionEn: extremely bad or serious
words.exampleEn: His injuries are severe.

words.definitionEn: very badly or seriously
words.exampleEn: severely injured

words.definitionEn: sexually attractive
words.exampleEn: the sexy lead singer

words.definitionEn: an area that is dark and cool under or behind something, for example a tree or building, because the sun’s light does not get to it
words.exampleEn: shade of something, The shade of the pine tree provided some protection from the sun.

words.definitionEn: the dark shape that somebody/something’s form makes on a surface, for example on the ground, when they are between the light and the surface
words.exampleEn: The children were having fun, chasing each other's shadows.

words.definitionEn: not having much distance between the top or surface and the bottom
words.exampleEn: a shallow dish/pan/bowl

words.definitionEn: used to say that something is a cause for feeling sad or disappointed
words.exampleEn: She's retiring because of ill health, which is a great shame.

words.definitionEn: to make something into a particular shape
words.exampleEn: shape A into B, Shape the dough into a ball.

words.definitionEn: having the type of shape mentioned
words.exampleEn: a huge balloon shaped like a giant cow

words.definitionEn: the fact of having a place to live or stay, considered as a basic human need
words.exampleEn: Human beings need food, clothing and shelter.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody/something a place where they are protected from the weather or from danger; to protect somebody/something
words.exampleEn: shelter somebody/something from somebody/something, Trees shelter the house from the wind.

words.definitionEn: to move, or move something, from one position or place to another
words.exampleEn: Lydia shifted uncomfortably in her chair.

words.definitionEn: to send or transport somebody/something by ship or by another means of transport
words.exampleEn: The company ships its goods all over the world.

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling of surprise as a result of something happening, especially something unpleasant; the event that causes this feeling
words.exampleEn: I got a terrible shock the other day.

words.definitionEn: to surprise and upset somebody
words.exampleEn: shock somebody, President Kennedy's assassination in Dallas on November 22, 1963, shocked the world.

words.definitionEn: surprised and upset; showing that somebody feels surprised and upset
words.exampleEn: I was quite shocked when I found out what he'd done.

words.definitionEn: that offends or upsets people; that is morally wrong
words.exampleEn: shocking behaviour

words.definitionEn: a situation in which a person is shot with a gun
words.exampleEn: Terrorist groups claimed responsibility for the shootings and bomb attacks.

words.definitionEn: the land along the edge of the sea, the ocean or a lake
words.exampleEn: a rocky/sandy shore

words.definitionEn: lasting a short time; designed only for a short period of time in the future
words.exampleEn: a short-term loan

words.definitionEn: a situation when there is not enough of the people or things that are needed
words.exampleEn: food/housing/water shortages

words.definitionEn: a short time; not long
words.exampleEn: She arrived shortly after us.

words.definitionEn: the act of firing a gun; the sound this makes
words.exampleEn: We heard some shots in the distance.

words.definitionEn: to be able to accept and deal with something unpleasant
words.exampleEn: bear something, The pain was almost more than he could bear.

words.definitionEn: a brother or sister
words.exampleEn: squabbles between siblings

words.definitionEn: your name as you usually write it, for example at the end of a letter
words.exampleEn: Someone had forged her signature on the cheque.

words.definitionEn: the importance of something, especially when this has an effect on what happens in the future
words.exampleEn: a decision of major political significance

words.definitionEn: large or important enough to have an effect or to be noticed
words.exampleEn: There are no significant differences between the two groups of students.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is large or important enough to have an effect on something or to be noticed
words.exampleEn: significantly higher/lower/larger/smaller

words.definitionEn: a complete lack of noise or sound
words.exampleEn: Their footsteps echoed in the silence.

words.definitionEn: fine soft thread produced by silkworms
words.exampleEn: The caterpillar spins the silk around its entire body.

words.definitionEn: a single hit to something, such as a drum, or a movement of something, such as your heart; the sound that this makes
words.exampleEn: several loud beats on the drum

words.definitionEn: showing what you really think or feel
words.exampleEn: a sincere apology

words.definitionEn: having enough ability, experience and knowledge to be able to do something well
words.exampleEn: a skilled engineer/negotiator/craftsman

words.definitionEn: the bone structure that forms the head and surrounds and protects the brain
words.exampleEn: a fractured skull

words.definitionEn: a person who is owned by another person and is forced to work for and obey them
words.exampleEn: A former slave, he graduated from Clafflin University in South Carolina.

words.definitionEn: one page of an electronic presentation, that may contain text and images, that is usually viewed on a computer screen or projected onto a larger screen
words.exampleEn: Here's a slide showing target markets.

words.definitionEn: to move easily over a smooth or wet surface; to make something move in this way
words.exampleEn: As I turned left on a bend, my car started to slide.

words.definitionEn: very small in degree
words.exampleEn: a slight increase/change/difference

words.definitionEn: to slide a short distance by accident so that you fall or nearly fall
words.exampleEn: She slipped and landed flat on her back.

words.definitionEn: a word or phrase that is easy to remember, used for example by a political party or in advertising to attract people’s attention or to suggest an idea quickly
words.exampleEn: an advertising slogan

words.definitionEn: a surface or piece of land that slopes (= is higher at one end than the other)
words.exampleEn: on a slope, The town is built on a slope.

words.definitionEn: to be at an angle so that it is higher at one end than the other
words.exampleEn: a sloping roof

words.definitionEn: used to show that you do not think that the word or phrase that is being used to describe somebody/something is appropriate
words.exampleEn: the opinion of a so-called ‘expert’

words.definitionEn: of or connected with the sun
words.exampleEn: solar radiation

words.definitionEn: in a way that is not known or certain
words.exampleEn: We must stop him from seeing her somehow.

words.definitionEn: the state of being exact or correct; the ability to do something with skill and without making mistakes
words.exampleEn: They questioned the accuracy of the information in the file.

words.definitionEn: at a time that you do not know exactly or has not yet been decided
words.exampleEn: I saw him sometime last summer.

words.definitionEn: to some degree
words.exampleEn: somewhat different/similar

words.definitionEn: clever and complicated in the way that it works or is presented
words.exampleEn: highly sophisticated computer systems

words.definitionEn: the spiritual part of a person, believed to exist after death
words.exampleEn: He believed his immortal soul was in peril.

words.definitionEn: available to do what you want with rather than work
words.exampleEn: He's studying music in his spare time.

words.definitionEn: to ask somebody for something especially in an anxious way because you want or need it very much
words.exampleEn: Now you have to beg and plead.

words.definitionEn: having or involving expert knowledge of a particular area of work, study or medicine
words.exampleEn: specialist knowledge/training/skills

words.definitionEn: a person who is an expert in a particular area of work or study
words.exampleEn: specialist in something, a specialist in Japanese history

words.definitionEn: to become an expert in a particular area of work, study or business; to spend more time on one area of work, etc. than on others
words.exampleEn: Many students prefer not to specialize too soon.

words.definitionEn: a group into which animals, plants, etc. that are able to have sex with each other and produce healthy young are divided, smaller than a genus and identified by a Latin name
words.exampleEn: a conservation area for endangered species

words.definitionEn: to state something, especially by giving an exact measurement, time, exact instructions, etc.
words.exampleEn: specify something, Remember to specify your size when ordering clothes.

words.definitionEn: very impressive
words.exampleEn: The coastal road has spectacular scenery.

words.definitionEn: a person who is watching an event, especially a sports event
words.exampleEn: The new football stadium will hold 75 000 spectators.

words.definitionEn: to form an opinion about something without knowing all the details or facts
words.exampleEn: speculate (about/on/as to something), We all speculated about the reasons for her resignation.

words.definitionEn: the act of forming opinions about what has happened or what might happen without knowing all the facts
words.exampleEn: His private life is the subject of much speculation.

words.definitionEn: to move along quickly
words.exampleEn: They sped off to get help.

words.definitionEn: one of the various types of powder or seed that come from plants and are used in cooking. Spices have a strong taste and smell.
words.exampleEn: common spices such as ginger and cinnamon

words.definitionEn: to flow over the edge of a container by accident; to make liquid do this
words.exampleEn: Water had spilled out of the bucket onto the floor.

words.definitionEn: connected with the human spirit, rather than the body or physical things
words.exampleEn: a spiritual experience

words.definitionEn: a feeling of wanting to hurt or upset somebody
words.exampleEn: out of spite, I'm sure he only said it out of spite.

words.definitionEn: an argument or disagreement that divides a group of people or makes somebody separate from somebody else
words.exampleEn: Ten years after their acrimonious split, the band has reformed.

words.definitionEn: to divide, or to make something divide, into two or more parts
words.exampleEn: split something, He was a member of the team that split the atom in 1932.

words.definitionEn: to change something good into something bad, unpleasant, etc.
words.exampleEn: Our camping trip was spoilt by bad weather.

words.definitionEn: a person who speaks for a group or an organization
words.exampleEn: a police spokesman

words.definitionEn: a person who speaks for a group or an organization
words.exampleEn: a council spokesperson

words.definitionEn: a woman who speaks for a group or an organization
words.exampleEn: a government spokeswoman

words.definitionEn: a person or company that pays for a radio or television programme, or for a concert or sporting event, usually in return for advertising
words.exampleEn: The programme is funded by a number of corporate sponsors.

words.definitionEn: to pay the costs of a particular event, programme, etc. as a way of advertising
words.exampleEn: Sports events are no longer sponsored by the tobacco industry.

words.definitionEn: financial support from a sponsor
words.exampleEn: a $50 million sponsorship deal

words.definitionEn: connected with sports
words.exampleEn: a major sporting event

words.definitionEn: to see or notice a person or thing, especially suddenly or when it is not easy to do so
words.exampleEn: spot somebody/something, Can you spot the difference between these two pictures?

words.definitionEn: an increase in the amount or number of something that there is, or in the area that is affected by something
words.exampleEn: measures to halt the spread of the disease

words.definitionEn: to move suddenly and with one quick movement in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: The cat crouched ready to spring.

words.definitionEn: fixed or steady; not likely to move, change or fail
words.exampleEn: The patient's condition is stable (= it is not getting worse).

words.definitionEn: correct and true in every detail
words.exampleEn: an accurate description/picture of something

words.definitionEn: to organize and present a play or an event for people to see
words.exampleEn: to stage a play/an event/an exhibition

words.definitionEn: a table or small shop with an open front that people sell things from, especially at a market
words.exampleEn: He works on a market stall in the Square.

words.definitionEn: the opinions that somebody has about something and expresses publicly
words.exampleEn: What is the newspaper's stance on the war?
